occurrence_sentence: Zapata AI's shares dropped significantly on its first day of public trading on NASDAQ at ZPTA.

interaction_phrase: Zapata Computing Holdings Inc., an artificial intelligence-slash-quantum startup
occurrence_sentence: Zapata Computing Holdings Inc., an artificial intelligence-slash-quantum startup, saw its shares crash by as much as 60% in a dramatic first day on the stock market, after merging with a blank-check firm headed by former IndyCar driver Michael Andretti, according to [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-04-01/andretti-s-ai-spac-deal-brings-little-cash-as-shares-tumble-54?utm_source=google&utm_medium=bd&cmpId=google&embedded-checkout=true&leadSource=uverify%20wall) and other media outlets.

It's important to note that the generative AI business is a rather recent foray for Zapata AI, which was originally focused on quantum computing. It spun out of Harvard in 2017, where its founders were pioneers in the field of near-term quantum algorithms, including the development of the Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE).
It spun out of Harvard in 2017, where its founders were pioneers in the field of near-term quantum algorithms, including the development of the Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE).
The Quantum Insider Intelligence platform still shows that Zapata AI is continuing work in quantum, including the recent granting of patents on quantum-related research.
The Quantum Insider Intelligence platform still shows that Zapata AI is continuing work in quantum, including the recent granting of patents on quantum-related research.
Lincoln Park Capital Fund is no stranger to backing quantum companies.

The investment group backed D-Wave Quantum in an agreement [to purchase up to $150 million of the quantum startup's options when it, too, had a rough SPAC start due to redemption rates](https://thequantuminsider.com/2022/08/09/d-wave-quantum-up-in-trading-secures-150-million-in-long-term-funding/) right before going public.
